bullets: Nine U.S. companies scanned over four weeks, drawing on SEC EDGAR filings and macroeconomic documents on GDP and inflation, framed around Kitchin business cycles.
gpt-4o was queried via API in a retrieval-augmented regime to generate automatic investor briefs; outputs were judged for usefulness rather than validated against any ground truth.
Briefs were distributed to nine individual investors to rate the usefulness of the approach; the abstract reports no quantitative accuracy or performance figure.
Conceptual framework paper for agentic AI model risk, illustrated with a single empirical case study and an ablation; the underlying dataset and period are not stated.
An unnamed large language model acts as a semantic observation model mapping evidence to a distribution over latent regimes, combined with a Bayesian filter for belief updating.
Bayesian filtering reduces posterior instability and uncertainty, improves regime-classification accuracy, and lowers downstream risk measures, with no numeric magnitude given in the abstract.
Financial PhraseBank corpus with a held-out test subset of 300 expert-annotated sentences, run on a single consumer-grade GPU.
DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B fine-tuned with LoRA and 4-bit QLoRA, evaluated against expert sentiment labels for accuracy and weighted F1.
Fine-tuned model reaches 84.33% accuracy and 84.17% weighted F1, versus 54.00% accuracy and 45.32% F1 for the zero-shot base model.
Stack Overflow questions surrounding the November 2022 launch of ChatGPT, analyzed through several empirical designs, with effects tracked into May 2023.
ChatGPT, version not stated, is treated as an external shock rather than a research tool; its causal effect is estimated with difference-in-differences style designs.
Question volume fell 14.09% on average and 27.88% by May 2023, while the average quality of remaining questions rose, driven partly by uneven substitution of simpler questions.
Methodological paper in strategic management, applied to a theory of strategic exploration under uncertainty; specific sample and data are not stated.

Proposes Generative AI-Based Experimentation, running unnamed LLMs as configurable in silico agents whose roles, preferences, and stated explanations can be varied, checked against prior human-subject results.
The framework reproduces human-subject experimental results at lower cost and extends theory by clarifying boundary conditions and surfacing mechanisms; no agreement statistic is reported.
Review article synthesizing the emerging literature at the intersection of generative AI and finance, organized around six areas spanning corporate, asset pricing, and household finance.
Not a model-using study; it surveys LLMs as analytic tools, external economic shocks, and autonomous economic agents, without applying or validating any specific model.
Maps findings across corporate finance, asset pricing, household finance, and labor economics, and identifies open questions and future research directions rather than a new empirical result.
One-shot Prisoner's Dilemma experiment using a full factorial design that varies payoff parameters, number of partners, strategy space, and elicitation order.
ChatGPT-3.5-turbo serves as the experimental subject, providing both decisions and stated beliefs; behavior is characterized rather than compared to a benchmark.
The model cooperates highly at baseline despite pessimistic beliefs, is largely insensitive to payoff variation, but responds systematically to group size, action space, and elicitation order.
Cryptocurrency markets and 32 global equity markets, using financial news, with recursive out-of-sample forecasting and economic value tests; the sample period is not stated.
Three Gemini-1.5-Flash agents process financial news to split disagreement into within-group belief dispersion and cross-group stance polarization; no ground-truth validation is reported.

Within-group uncertainty predicts downward price pressure while cross-group polarization predicts higher subsequent returns; crypto disagreement leads international equity returns with meaningful certainty-equivalent gains.
Survey of the emerging Agentic FinTech paradigm, spanning application domains such as trading, risk management, and regulatory compliance, with no empirical sample.
Reviews LLM-based financial agents with perception, planning, memory, and tool use; no single model is deployed or validated by the authors.
Formalizes core agentic capabilities and organizes workflows, architectures, and evaluation methods, outlining open challenges toward trustworthy agentic financial systems, with no empirical finding.
41,332 attorney-coded police stop narratives from 2012 to 2023 in one jurisdiction, plus more than one million additional stops with imputed legality.
Fine-tuned GPT-4.1 classifies whether a stop is illegal from its narrative, validated against attorney coding at over 90% accuracy with well-calibrated confidence.
Officer heterogeneity is limited (replacing the most illegal half of officers cuts illegal stops only 12.7%), and stops of Black males are 1.6 points more likely illegal.
